channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Fuze review: this explosive new thriller movie will keep you on the edge of your seat — but wastes its all-star cast
item.description: Fuze is a fun, fast-paced thriller but it might leave you wanting a little more.

item.title: AI agents are learning on the job — just not for your whole team
item.description: When someone on a team corrects an AI agent — better prompts, better feedback, better context — that improvement disappears the moment a colleague opens the same tool. The correction doesn't transfer, and the next person starts from zero.The problem compounds in multi-agent workflows, where teams expect agents to share context across users and tasks. Without a shared memory layer, every team member effectively trains a different version of the same agent — and those versions never sync.That gap shows up in the numbers. According to Asana's own research, 75% of knowledge workers use AI on the j...

item.title: GM’s electric future depends on a new battery — and this building
item.description: GM wants to slash EV prices by deploying new battery tech up to a year earlier than planned. This building is key to making that happen.

item.title: As VC-backed e-bike startups went bankrupt, bootstrapped Lectric grew
item.description: Lectric, which says the U.S. market is ripe for competition and choice, has launched three new brands in the past six months.

item.title: Prime Day is right around the corner: Here are 20+ early deals to start shopping now
item.description: Amazon Prime Day is coming June 23. Shop early Prime Day deals on Sony headphones, Hisense TVs, and Anker power stations.

item.title: ‘This is a fundamental shift in how we prepare for pandemics’: Researchers used AI and known genetic codes to develop a ‘fundamentally new’ vaccine that ‘could help speed up the roll out vaccines to benefit people all over the world’
item.description: The research team hope that the new AI development method could help create vaccines that target a whole family of viruses

item.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
item.description: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction. Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on bringing people together through in-person games and social experiences. Cyberdeck creators are going viral crafting whimsical DIY computers that literally encourage users to touch grass. Unlike the AI-free browser crowd, this doesn’t just feel like backlash, […]...

item.title: Hackers abused Stripe and Google Tag Manager to launch a credit card theft campaign and host stolen payment details
item.description: Google Tag Manager is also abused in this campaign.

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5000 Super GPU refreshes could arrive in 2026 after all — with a surprise addition that won't destroy your wallet like the others
item.description: Has Nvidia changed its mind about RTX Super GPU refreshes for Blackwell? Apparently, although I remain skeptical...

item.title: This year's PC Gaming Show will provide looks at more than 50 games and also feature a behind-the-scenes look at one of my most anticipated games of the year — here's how to tune in
item.description: This year's PC Gaming Show is nearly here, where we'll get a look at more than 50 games.

item.title: The Sonos Era 100 speaker is down to its lowest price in months
item.description: Whether you’re considering starting a Sonos speaker setup, or adding to an existing group, the Sonos Era 100 is worth picking up. The compact, capable smart speaker is currently marked down to $189 ($30 off) at a variety of retailers, including Amazon, Best Buy, and directly from Sonos. If you want an even lower price, […]

item.title: Apple says these malls got so bad it’s leaving
item.description: Three Apple Stores will close this month as the company cites worsening conditions at their shopping centers. The countdown is on for Apple’s devoted flock: Later this month, the tech giant will permanently close three stores in California, Connecticut, and Maryland.

item.title: DaVinci Resolve 21 (2026) review: Our top free video editing app gets big improvements and a new Lightroom-style Photos tool for color-grading images
item.description: DaVinci Resolve has just released its latest version: 21, and with it comes a brand new Page, dedicated to Photos. Does it provide the tools you need to work with images inside a video project, or on their own?

item.title: One of my all-time favourite JRPGs has been announced for the Xbox Game Pass lineup for June ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase
item.description: Microsoft has announced the Xbox Game Pass lineup for the first half of June ahead of the 2026 Xbox Games Showcase.

item.title: The 11 best headphones, speakers, soundbars, and streamers we saw at the High End Vienna 2026 hi-fi show
item.description: From one of the best speakers in the world becoming even better, to a TV sound system that has genuine audiophile skills, to wireless speakers that look like alien invaders

item.title: Google shuts down the AI image app Pixel Studio
item.description: Google's AI app Pixel Studio launched less than two years ago, but is now being shut down.

item.title: Sony DualSense Edge, Samsung Odyssey G6 et pack Nothing Phone (3) : les 3 meilleurs bons plans tech du jour (vendredi 5 juin 2026)
item.description: La fin de semaine est souvent synonyme de remises intéressantes, et ce vendredi 5 juin 2026 ne déroge pas à la règle.

item.title: Volvo EX60, Hyundai Ioniq 5 N, MG4 Urban : nos 3 voitures électriques préférées du mois
item.description: Trois voitures électriques, trois philosophies opposées : le SUV premium suédois bardé d'IA, la sportive coréenne de 650 chevaux et la familiale chinoise à moins de 20 000 euros. Voici nos trois coups de cœur du mois.

item.title: Meta's AI support agent bound recovery emails for anyone who asked. Your SOC never saw an alert.
item.description: Meta's AI support agent bound recovery emails to accounts for whoever asked, and SOCs never saw an alert. An authorized agent writes a log of legitimate transactions, so nothing in the detection stack fired. Attackers asked the bot to make the change, took the one-time code it sent, and ran the password reset, 404 Media reported.No malware, no stolen credentials, and no prompt injection in the sense most security teams drill for. The agent did exactly what Meta built it to do. That is what should keep a security operations leader up at night: The takeover did not break a control; it rode one t...

item.title: This is your laptop… on AI
item.description: We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything. Nvidia's Jensen Huang made that clearer than anyone this week, when he described a completely new way of using our laptops […]

item.title: Russian Roskomnadzor accused of launching active DDoS attacks on VPN services — here's what we know so far
item.description: Russia's media regulator, Roskomnadzor, has allegedly shifted from blocking VPNs to actively launching DDoS attacks against their infrastructure, leaving services like Amnezia VPN struggling to stay online.

item.title: Supabase doubles valuation to $10B in 8 months
item.description: Supabase, an example of an open source project becoming a fast-growing company, has greatly benefited from AI tools like Claude, Codex, and other vibe-coding platforms.

item.title: The early Prime Day appliance deals worth shopping right now — record-low prices from Ninja, Shark, Tineco and Instant Pot
item.description: As a professional deals hunter, I've rounded up my favorite early Prime Day appliance offers, including up to 44% from brands like Ninja, Shark, Tineco, and Instant Pot.

item.title: Ahead of Prime Day, the Igloo Kool Tunes boombox cooler is on sale for $40 off
item.description: The Igloo Kool Tunes (red) is on sale at Amazon for $99.99, down from the normal price of $139.99. That's a 29% discount.

item.title: How to showcase your expertise as a strategist, coach, or consultant
item.description: No tangible output? You can still demonstrate results. As a freelance writer, I have it easy. When someone asks for samples of my work, I can send them a link. My skills are right there on the page, with my name on it.

item.title: Meta is building AI data centers in tents
item.description: Meta is building out data centers inside weatherproof tents to speed up construction.

item.title: What happens when your phone is confiscated at the airport
item.description: Even if you've done nothing wrong, it's never a good idea to hand your phone to the cops. But international travelers at American airports often have no choice - even if they're US citizens. When Minnesota labor organizer Janette Zahia Corcelius returned home from a three-week trip to Europe in late April, she was detained […]

item.title: NASAs ISS astronauts briefly sheltered today. Heres what happened.
item.description: NASA crew shelters in Dragon as Russia halts ISS Zvezda crack repairs, highlighting aging station leaks and joint safety efforts.

item.title: Grab the Ecovacs Goat O1000 robot lawn mower while its $300 off
item.description: The Ecovacs Goat O1000 RTK robot lawn mower is down to just $699.99 at Amazon. That's $300 off and its best price ever.

item.title: Logitech G512 X 98 Review: A Hybrid Mish-Mash
item.description: The Logitech G512 X 98 lets you swap between mechanical and analog switches in an attempt to achieve the best of both worlds. Unfortunately, its solution isn't as well thought-out as I'd hoped.

item.title: Google and FBI warn of ransomware group that sends fake IT workers to hack victims in person
item.description: Cybercriminals, part of a gang known as Silent Ransom Group, have sent people pretending to be IT support employees to law firms' offices, where the criminals have stolen data using USB drives or remote access tools.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 hype kicks off fraud, fake apps, and ransomware targeting fans and businesses
item.description: Hackers have been preparing longer than the footballers and the scam infrastructure is now up and running.
